itineraries.name: Haro and the Barrio de la Estacion weekend
itineraries.theme: Century-old Haro houses: the classic Rioja heartland

days.morning: Start in Haro's Barrio de la Estacion, the world's densest cluster of century-old bodegas, with a morning cellar tour and tasting at La Rioja Alta, founded 1890, focused on Vina Ardanza and the Gran Reservas.
days.lunch: Lunch in Haro's old town off the Plaza de la Paz between cellar visits.
days.afternoon: Cross the street for an afternoon visit at CVNE, founded 1879, to taste Imperial and Vina Real, then a shorter stop at Gomez Cruzado in the same district.
days.evening: Dinner at Los Canos in Haro's former jail and town hall, with a local Rioja list under original stone and timber.
days.accommodation: Stay in central Haro within walking distance of the station district.

days.morning: Begin with the temple of traditional Rioja, Lopez de Heredia, founded 1877, for the Vina Tondonia cellars and a tasting of patiently aged whites and reds.
days.lunch: Picnic or a light lunch in Haro before the afternoon visit.
days.afternoon: Finish at Bodegas Muga for its cooperage and oak-fermentation tour, tasting the Reserva and Prado Enea, then browse the Barrio shops for bottles to take home.
days.evening: Relaxed evening back in Haro with a final glass of Reserva at a town-centre bar.
days.accommodation: Second night in Haro or onward to Logrono.

itineraries.description: A two-day weekend through Haro's Barrio de la Estacion, the world's largest concentration of century-old bodegas, visiting La Rioja Alta, CVNE, Gomez Cruzado, Lopez de Heredia and Muga.
itineraries.tip: Cellars in the Barrio book out, especially in June; reserve each visit a couple of weeks ahead and keep the walking distances short.

itineraries.name: Rioja Alavesa architecture and terroir

days.morning: Base in the walled hill town of Laguardia and open with Marques de Riscal at Elciego, founded 1858, touring the Frank Gehry City of Wine and tasting the classic Reservas.
days.lunch: Lunch at the estate or in Elciego village below the Gehry hotel.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Bodegas Ysios to see the Santiago Calatrava cellar set against the Sierra de Cantabria, with a tasting of its Reserva.
days.evening: Dinner and an evening stroll inside the Laguardia walls.
days.accommodation: Boutique stay in Laguardia old town.

days.morning: Visit Artadi outside Laguardia, organically farmed and certified-organic from the 2016 vintage, for its single-vineyard alta-expresion Tempranillo.
days.lunch: Lunch in Laguardia between the morning and afternoon estates.
days.afternoon: Continue to Remirez de Ganuza at Samaniego for a tour of its meticulous grape-selection cellar and a tasting of the Reserva.
days.evening: Aperitivo back in Laguardia before dinner.

days.morning: Head to Luis Canas at Villabuena de Alava, a benchmark Alavesa family house, for its range from Crianza up to the single-vineyard wines.
days.lunch: Lunch in Villabuena, one of Rioja's densest village concentrations of bodegas.
days.afternoon: Finish at Vinedos del Contino near Laserna, a walled single-estate on the Ebro, tasting the estate Reserva and the Vina del Olivo.
days.evening: Drive to Logrono for a final night on Calle San Juan.

itineraries.description: Three days in Rioja Alavesa from Laguardia, pairing the Gehry and Calatrava signature-architecture estates with single-vineyard houses Artadi, Remirez de Ganuza, Luis Canas and Contino.
itineraries.tip: Riscal and Ysios both need timed bookings; cluster the Villabuena and Samaniego visits since the villages sit only minutes apart.

itineraries.name: Logrono base and Rioja Alta grand tour
itineraries.theme: City pintxos plus the big Rioja Alta names

days.morning: Base in Logrono and start at Marques de Murrieta at Ygay, heir to Rioja's first commercial bodega, for the estate tour and the Castillo Ygay story.
days.lunch: Lunch at the estate or back in central Logrono.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Campo Viejo on the Logrono outskirts for its large-scale, design-forward cellar and an accessible tasting.
days.evening: Pintxos crawl along Calle Laurel and Calle San Juan, finishing with by-the-glass Rioja at Tastavin.

days.morning: Drive west into Rioja Alta to Finca Allende at Briones for Miguel Angel de Gregorio's precise, terroir-led wines.
days.lunch: Lunch in Briones, home to the Vivanco wine museum.
days.afternoon: Spend the afternoon at the Vivanco Museum of the Culture of Wine and its estate, one of the best wine museums in the world.
days.evening: Return to Logrono for dinner and a glass at Wine Fandango.

days.morning: Head to Sierra Cantabria at San Vicente de la Sonsierra, an Eguren-family benchmark, for its old-vine Tempranillo.
days.lunch: Lunch in San Vicente de la Sonsierra overlooking the Ebro.
days.afternoon: Finish with a visit at Bodegas Roda back in Haro's Barrio de la Estacion before heading home.
days.evening: Last glass in Haro or on the road.
days.accommodation: Departure or final night in Logrono.

itineraries.description: Three days based in Logrono, combining the city's pintxos-and-wine streets with the big Rioja Alta names: Murrieta, Campo Viejo, Finca Allende, the Vivanco museum, Sierra Cantabria and Roda.
itineraries.tip: Logrono makes an easy base with short drives out each day; book Murrieta and Finca Allende ahead and leave time for the Vivanco museum.
